perturbation.original_text: 1.1.2 Party A owns the sole and exclusive right of the intellectual property, including any improvement, upgrades and derived products, no matter whether such products are created by Party A or Party B. The right and obligation under 1.1.2 of this article shall survive upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1.2 Party A owns the sole and exclusive right of the intellectual property, including any improvement, upgrades and derived products, no matter whether such products are created by Party A or Party B. Party B shall retain ownership of any improvements, upgrades, and derived products created solely by Party B. The right and obligation under 1.1.2 of this article shall survive upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the ownership of intellectual property improvements. Initially, Party A owns everything. The modification states Party B owns improvements they create, creating a conflict. This contradicts with the initial statment where 'Party A owns the sole and exclusive right of the intellectual property, including any improvement, upgrades and derived products, no matter whether such products are created by Party A or Party B'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1.2 Party A owns the sole and exclusive right of the intellectual property, including any improvement, upgrades and derived products, no matter whether such products are created by Party A or Party B. Party B shall retain ownership of any improvements, upgrades, and derived products created solely by Party B. The right and obligation under 1.1.2 of this article shall survive upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. Initially, 1.1.2 states that Party A owns all intellectual property, including improvements, regardless of who creates them. The changed version adds that Party B retains ownership of improvements it creates. This directly conflicts with the initial statement assigning all ownership to Party A. The contradicted text and changed text are the same location, further highlighting the in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Party B agrees to pay Party A the Intellectual Property License fees (the "License Fees") and the specified amount of the license fees and the form of payment are set forth in Appendix 2. Party A has the right to exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount of the License Fees set forth in Appendix 2 according to the actual circumstances from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B agrees to pay Party A the Intellectual Property usage Fees (the "License Fees") and the specified amount of the license fees and the form of payment are set forth in Appendix 2. Party A has the right to exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount of the Royalties set forth in Appendix 2 according to the actual circumstances from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text consistently refers to 'License Fees.' The modification changes 'Intellectual Property License fees' to 'Intellectual Property usage Fees' and then 'License Fees' to 'Royalties.' This introduces uncertainty about the exact nature and calculation of the fees owed. It creates contradiction on wether the 'License Fees' are the same as 'Royalties', or 'Intellectual Property usage Fees'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B agrees to pay Party A the Intellectual Property usage Fees (the "License Fees") and the specified amount of the license fees and the form of payment are set forth in Appendix 2. Party A has the right to exempt the obligation of payment or adjust the amount of the Royalties set forth in Appendix 2 according to the actual circumstances from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces 'Intellectual Property usage Fees' and 'Royalties' as potentially distinct from 'License Fees' where the contradicted text uses these terms seemingly interchangeably. This creates a direct conflict in defining the payment obligations, making it unclear which fees apply and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as it could cause confusion about payment obligations and potentially lead to disputes over which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Party B shall protect and maintain the confidentiality of any and all confidential data and information acknowledged or received by Party B from Party A (collectively the "Confidential Information"). Upon termination of this Agreement, Party B shall return Confidential Information to Party A or destroy it itself and delete Confidential Information from any electronic devices and cease to use them as required by Party A. Party B shall not disclose, grant or transfer any Confidential Information to any third party and will not use the Confidential Information without Party A's written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Party B shall protect and maintain the confidentiality of any and all sensitive data and information acknowledged or received by Party B from Party A (collectively the "Confidential Information"). Upon termination of this Agreement, Party B shall return Private Information to Party A or destroy it itself and delete Private Information from any electronic devices and cease to use them as required by Party A. Party B shall not disclose, grant or transfer any Confidential Information to any third party and will not use the Sensitive Information without Party A's written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text consistently refers to 'Confidential Information'. The modification changes 'confidential data and information' to 'sensitive data and information', 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information', and 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information'. This introduces uncertainty about what types of information are truly protected under the confidentiality clause. The text refers to Private Information, Confidential Information, and Sensitive Information without clarifying if they are the same or distinct, weakening the enforceability of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Party B shall protect and maintain the confidentiality of any and all sensitive data and information acknowledged or received by Party B from Party A (collectively the "Confidential Information"). Upon termination of this Agreement, Party B shall return Private Information to Party A or destroy it itself and delete Private Information from any electronic devices and cease to use them as required by Party A. Party B shall not disclose, grant or transfer any Confidential Information to any third party and will not use the Sensitive Information without Party A's written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the terms 'Private Information' and 'Sensitive Information' alongside 'Confidential Information' without defining their relationship. This creates ambiguity about which type of information Party B is obligated to protect, directly contradicting the intent of maintaining consistent terminology and creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined in Misaligned Terminology, making the enforcement of the clause unclear. The contradiction is strong because it refers to different terms in the same clause, causing uncertainty and ambiguity.
